step_description: Top SQL by elapsed time delta between AWR snapshots
rows: UPDATE ORDERS SET status='PROCESSED' WHERE order_id=:1
SELECT * FROM ORDERS WHERE customer_id=:1 AND status=:2
narrative_hint: SQL 7tqm4xwjnb2dp executed 48,204 times — one execution per order processed by BATCH_ORDER_PROC. Each execution commits individually (confirmed by matching log file sync count of 48,420). The UPDATE + INSERT + sequence SELECT pattern shows BATCH_ORDER_PROC is processing one order at a time with an individual commit per order. Changing to batch commits of 500-1000 rows would reduce log file sync waits by ~99%. Claude should provide a specific recommendation to add batch commit logic to BATCH_ORDER_PROC and estimate the performance improvement.
